# OpenSIN Documentation

> Canonical docs, governance, and surface registry for the OpenSIN-AI ecosystem.

## Overview

OpenSIN Documentation is the VitePress-powered knowledge base for the OpenSIN-AI ecosystem. It centralizes guides, architecture notes, APIs, governance, and evidence-based surface status for humans and agents.

## Site map

- Docs: https://docs.opensin.ai
- Main site: https://opensin.ai
- Marketplace: https://my.opensin.ai
- Blog: https://blog.opensin.ai
- Registry: https://docs.opensin.ai/governance/domain-registry.html

## Documentation map

- Guide: https://docs.opensin.ai/guide/getting-started.html
- Architecture: https://docs.opensin.ai/architecture/overview.html
- API: https://docs.opensin.ai/api/overview.html
- Governance: https://docs.opensin.ai/governance/overview.html
- Best Practices: https://docs.opensin.ai/best-practices/index.html
- Tutorials: https://docs.opensin.ai/tutorials/first-agent.html
- Examples: https://docs.opensin.ai/examples/hello-world.html
- Bridges: https://docs.opensin.ai/bridges/index.html
- SDK: https://docs.opensin.ai/sdk/overview.html
- Plugins: https://docs.opensin.ai/plugins/registry.html

## Surface snapshot

- Public: opensin.ai, my.opensin.ai, blog.opensin.ai, docs.opensin.ai
- Gated: chat.opensin.ai
- Internal/unverified: api.opensin.ai, opensin.ai/agents

## Local workflow

```bash
git clone https://github.com/OpenSIN-AI/OpenSIN-documentation.git
cd OpenSIN-documentation
bun install
bun run docs:dev
```

## Governance notes

- Keep claims evidence-based.
- Update the registry before public-facing copy changes.
- Keep `llms.txt` and `llms-full.txt` in sync with the docs map.
- Prefer small, surgical documentation changes.

## Extended references

- What's New: https://docs.opensin.ai/guide/whats-new.html
- Security best practices: https://docs.opensin.ai/best-practices/security.html
- Performance best practices: https://docs.opensin.ai/best-practices/performance.html
